title: lomax punctulata
intro: Lomax punctulata, also known as Lomatium punctulatum, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States and Canada. It belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant contains sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and coumarins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, providing health benefits like reducing fever and relieving congestion. Herbal preparations of Lomax punctulata can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
